method: wall-clock via curl (bash). Includes prefill, decode, and HTTP overhead. Streaming TTFT/TPOT not captured in this first pass — see bench_vllm.py for proper streaming measurement.
prompt_text: Describe a pedestrian crossing scene in an autonomous driving context. Include vehicles, pedestrians, signals, and potential hazards.
note_on_sampling: vLLM applied embedl's generation_config.json defaults (temp=0.7, top_k=20, top_p=0.8). Not temperature=0; subsequent runs with deterministic sampling will differ slightly.
notes: Wall TPS includes prefill (TTFT). True decode-only TPS is higher; bench_vllm.py streaming will separate TTFT and TPOT.
max_model_len=256 is hard constraint of this serve config. llama.cpp runs at ctx=4096 for comparison — asymmetry documented in blog.
Result is consistent with Embedl's reported 16-17 TPS on Orin Nano Super and AdwaitX independent measurement.
Image-input benchmark not captured in this first pass — ViT encoder path adds ~260 image tokens which eats context budget.
